scope.summary:
Goal: Implement the buffer-transform handlers behind the MT-010 keymap line-edit actions that currently resolve to actions whose handler bodies do not exist. MT-010 (`src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/keymap.rs`) already declares the `CodeEditorAction` variants `ToggleComment`, `DuplicateLine`, `MoveLineUp`, `MoveLineDown`, `DeleteLine`, `IndentLine`, `DedentLine`, and `InsertTab`, and binds them to VS Code parity chords (Ctrl+/, Ctrl+Shift+K, Alt+Up, Alt+Down, Tab, etc.). When `Keymap::resolve` returns one of these actions and `mod.rs` dispatches it, the handler is currently a stub / unimplemented branch. This MT supplies the real buffer transforms so those chords actually edit the document. Without it, Toggle Comment (Ctrl+/) and the line-edit chords are dead keys.

Expected diff shape: (a) one new module `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/line_ops.rs` (~300-450 lines) that defines pure functions operating on `TextBuffer` (MT-001) and `CursorSet` (MT-003); (b) a small edit to `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/keymap.rs` ONLY if a comment-token / tab-policy lookup helper needs to live next to the action enum (prefer adding the lookup to line_ops.rs to keep keymap.rs binding-only — see notes); (c) the dispatch wiring edit in `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/mod.rs`'s `process_keymap`-driven match so the eight actions call into `line_ops`; (d) a `cursor.rs` touch ONLY if `CursorSet` lacks a helper this MT needs (e.g. a `lines_touched()` accessor) — keep this minimal and additive, do not refactor the cursor model. There is NO backend involvement: `binds_backend_api = ["NONE"]`.

Exact files to CREATE/EDIT:
- CREATE `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/line_ops.rs` — the transform library. Define these public functions, each taking `&mut TextBuffer`, `&mut CursorSet`, and a `&LineEditContext` (language + tab settings), and each producing exactly ONE coalesced undo entry (see undo note below):
  * `toggle_comment(buf, cursors, ctx)` — language-aware line-comment toggle.
  * `duplicate_line(buf, cursors, ctx)` — duplicate the line (or each selected line) below.
  * `move_line_up(buf, cursors, ctx)` and `move_line_down(buf, cursors, ctx)` — swap the cursor's line with the line above/below, moving cursors with the text.
  * `delete_line(buf, cursors, ctx)` — delete the whole row including its trailing newline; place the cursor at the start of the next row (or end of buffer).
  * `indent_line(buf, cursors, ctx)` and `dedent_line(buf, cursors, ctx)` — add/remove one indent unit at line start.
  * `insert_tab(buf, cursors, ctx)` — insert a tab character OR `tab_size` spaces depending on `ctx.insert_spaces`; when a selection spans multiple lines, Tab indents the block and Shift+Tab (routed to `dedent_line`) dedents it (VS Code parity).
  Define `LineEditContext { language: EditorLanguage, tab_size: usize, insert_spaces: bool }` and `EditorLanguage` (reuse / re-export the language enum already used by the MT-001 `LanguageRegistry`; do NOT define a second competing language enum — import it). Define `fn line_comment_token(language: EditorLanguage) -> Option<&'static str>` returning `"//"` for Rust/JS/TS/C/C++/Java/Go, `"#"` for Python/Shell/TOML/YAML, `"--"` for SQL/Lua, and `None` for languages with no line comment (those become a no-op for ToggleComment).
- EDIT `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/mod.rs` — in the action dispatch (the `match action { ... }` fed by `process_keymap` from MT-010), wire the eight variants to the corresponding `line_ops` calls, passing a `LineEditContext` built from the panel's current language and the operator's tab settings. Remove any prior placeholder/`todo!()`/no-op arms for these eight actions. Build the `LineEditContext` once per dispatch batch.
- EDIT `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/cursor.rs` — ONLY IF needed: add an additive helper such as `CursorSet::distinct_lines(buf: &TextBuffer) -> Vec<usize>` (sorted, de-duplicated set of line indices any cursor touches) used to apply a transform once per affected line. Do not change existing `Cursor`/`CursorSet` semantics.

Grounded API facts (verified against MT-001 and MT-003 contracts, do not re-derive):
- `TextBuffer` (MT-001, `buffer.rs`) exposes: `insert(byte_offset: usize, text: &str)`, `delete(byte_range: Range<usize>)`, `slice_to_string(line_range: Range<usize>) -> String`, `len_bytes() -> usize`, `len_lines() -> usize`, `line_to_byte(line_idx: usize) -> usize`, `byte_to_line(byte_offset: usize) -> usize`. Range ops are clamp/Result-safe (no panic on out-of-range). Use `line_to_byte`/`byte_to_line` for all row math; never hand-scan for newlines.
- `CursorSet` (MT-003, `cursor.rs`): `Cursor { anchor: usize, head: usize }` (byte offsets), `CursorSet { cursors: Vec<Cursor> }`, methods `add_cursor`, `remove_overlap()`, `move_all`, `insert_at_all`, `delete_at_all`. Cursors are kept sorted by `head`; after any mutation call `remove_overlap()` then re-sort.
- `CodeEditorAction` variants this MT implements are already declared in MT-010 `keymap.rs`: `ToggleComment`, `DuplicateLine`, `MoveLineUp`, `MoveLineDown`, `DeleteLine`, `IndentLine`, `DedentLine`, `InsertTab`. Do NOT add new enum variants; only implement handlers.

Step-by-step implementation notes (multi-cursor + undo are the two cross-cutting requirements):
1. MULTI-CURSOR MODEL: every transform must apply per affected line, derived from the cursor set, not just the primary cursor. Compute the distinct, sorted set of line indices touched by all cursors (each cursor's selection may span multiple lines — include every line from `byte_to_line(min(anchor,head))` to `byte_to_line(max(anchor,head))`). De-duplicate so two cursors on the same line transform it once.
2. APPLY BOTTOM-UP: when a transform changes byte lengths (comment insert/remove, duplicate, move, delete, indent), process the affected lines in DESCENDING line order so earlier (lower-offset) line positions stay valid while later lines are mutated. After all edits, recompute/adjust every cursor's `anchor`/`head` so the cursor stays on the same logical line/column it started on (e.g. after ToggleComment that added `// `, shift heads on that line right by the inserted byte count; after MoveLineUp, move the head's byte offset by the delta of the swapped line lengths). Then call `cursors.remove_overlap()` and re-sort.
3. TOGGLE COMMENT: resolve `token = line_comment_token(ctx.language)`; if `None`, no-op. For each affected line, read the line text via `slice_to_string(line..line+1)`. Determine the line's first non-whitespace column. VS Code semantics: if EVERY affected non-blank line is already commented (first non-ws chars == token), then UNCOMMENT all (remove the token plus one optional following space); otherwise COMMENT all (insert `token + " "` at the first non-whitespace column, or at column 0 for a blank line per VS Code, keeping a consistent insert column across the block = the minimum first-non-ws column of the block). Use `line_to_byte` + the column offset to compute the exact byte position for `insert`/`delete`.
4. DUPLICATE LINE: for each affected line (descending), read its full text including no trailing newline via slice, then `insert` a copy plus a leading/trailing newline immediately after the line's end byte (`line_to_byte(line+1)` is the start of the next line / insertion point; for the last line with no trailing newline, prepend a `\n`). Cursor stays on the original line (VS Code keeps the cursor on the lower/new copy — match VS Code: cursor moves to the duplicated line, i.e. head shifts down by one line's byte length).
5. MOVE LINE UP/DOWN: swap line L with L-1 (up) or L+1 (down). Read both line texts, delete the two-line span, re-insert in swapped order via a single `delete` + `insert` pair (so it coalesces). Clamp: MoveLineUp on line 0 is a no-op; MoveLineDown on the last line is a no-op. Move the cursor with its line (head byte offset adjusted by the neighbor line's length delta).
6. DELETE LINE: for each affected line (descending), delete the byte range `[line_to_byte(line) .. line_to_byte(line+1)]` (this includes the trailing newline). For the last line, delete `[line_to_byte(line)-1 .. len_bytes()]` to also drop the preceding newline so no empty trailing line remains. After deletion, set the affected cursor head to `line_to_byte(min(line, len_lines().saturating_sub(1)))`.
7. INDENT / DEDENT: one indent unit = a literal tab `\t` if `ctx.insert_spaces == false`, else `ctx.tab_size` spaces. INDENT inserts the unit at the start byte (`line_to_byte(line)`) of each affected line. DEDENT removes up to one leading indent unit: if the line starts with `\t` remove it; else remove up to `tab_size` leading spaces (fewer if the line has fewer). Multi-line selection + Tab = indent block (call indent on each affected line); Shift+Tab = dedent block.
8. INSERT TAB (single/collapsed cursor case): if there is NO multi-line selection, insert one indent unit (tab or `tab_size` spaces per `ctx.insert_spaces`) at each cursor head via the cursor set, then advance heads by the inserted byte length. If ANY cursor has a multi-line selection, route to `indent_line` block-indent instead (VS Code behavior: Tab with a block selection indents the block rather than replacing it).
9. UNDO COALESCING: each public `line_ops` function must group all of its sub-edits (across all affected lines and all cursors) into exactly ONE undo entry. The undo stack lives in the editor (introduced by the buffer/edit layer; if a `begin_undo_group()`/`end_undo_group()` or transaction API exists on `TextBuffer`/the panel, wrap the whole transform in it; if no such API exists yet, that is a TYPED BLOCKER — record it as `proof_targets`/`implementation_notes` blocker `BLOCKER-UNDO-API` and implement against the agreed shim rather than inventing a parallel undo stack). Do not emit one undo entry per line.

Dependencies / unblock conditions:
- MT-001 DONE (TextBuffer byte-offset API exists).
- MT-003 DONE (CursorSet multi-cursor model exists).
- MT-010 DONE (keymap.rs declares the eight CodeEditorAction variants and dispatches them through mod.rs).
- No new crates required: tabs/spaces/comment tokens are plain string ops on the rope. Do NOT add a dependency for this.

Blocks: MT-044 (depends on the line-edit transforms being real before it can build on them).

Risk if missed: the keymap declares actions (Ctrl+/ ToggleComment, Ctrl+Shift+K DeleteLine, Alt+Up/Down MoveLine, Tab IndentLine, etc.) whose handlers do not exist, so those chords are silently dead — a direct, visible VS Code parity regression.
acceptance_criteria: AC-001: `cargo test -p handshake-native toggle_comment` passes — a single Rust line gets a `// ` prefix toggled ON then OFF; a multi-cursor set toggles each touched line; a block where all lines are already commented uncomments all; a mixed block (some commented, some not) comments all.

AC-002: `cargo test -p handshake-native move_line` passes — MoveLineUp and MoveLineDown preserve the moved line's exact content, move the cursor with the line, and are no-ops at the top/bottom boundary respectively.
AC-003: `cargo test -p handshake-native duplicate_line` passes — DuplicateLine copies the line content verbatim onto a new adjacent line and leaves the cursor on the duplicated line (VS Code parity).

AC-004: `cargo test -p handshake-native indent_dedent` passes — IndentLine/DedentLine respect `tab_size` and the `insert_spaces` setting (tab char vs N spaces), dedent removes at most one indent unit, dedent on an already-flush line is a safe no-op.

AC-005: `cargo test -p handshake-native insert_tab` passes — InsertTab on a collapsed cursor inserts a tab or `tab_size` spaces per setting; InsertTab on a multi-line selection block-indents instead of replacing the selection.

AC-006: `cargo test -p handshake-native delete_line` passes — DeleteLine removes the whole row including its trailing newline, leaves no empty trailing line when the last row is deleted, and places the cursor at the start of the next row.

AC-007: Each transform produces exactly ONE coalesced undo entry — a test performs a multi-cursor transform then a single Undo and asserts the buffer returns to its exact pre-transform state in one step.
AC-008: ToggleComment on a language with no line comment token (`line_comment_token` returns None) is a safe no-op and leaves the buffer unchanged.
AC-009: After every transform `cursors.remove_overlap()` is called and the cursor set remains sorted by head with no duplicate or overlapping cursors.

AC-010: `cargo test -p handshake-native` passes with zero failures across all line_ops tests, and no `unwrap()`/`todo!()`/`unimplemented!()` remains on the eight action handler paths in mod.rs or line_ops.rs.
proof_targets: PT-001: `cargo test -p handshake-native line_ops -- --nocapture` => all line_ops tests GREEN, no panics, output logged.
PT-002: `cargo test -p handshake-native toggle_comment move_line duplicate_line delete_line indent_dedent insert_tab` => all named groups GREEN.
PT-003: undo-coalescing test: apply a multi-cursor ToggleComment across 3 lines, call Undo once, assert buffer string equals the pre-transform string exactly.

PT-004: grep proof — `ripgrep` on `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/mod.rs` and `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/line_ops.rs` for `todo!|unimplemented!` on the eight action arms returns 0 matches (handlers are real).

BLOCKER-UNDO-API (typed blocker, only if triggered): if no buffer transaction / undo-group API exists from a prior MT, record this as a typed blocker with the exact missing API signature needed (e.g. `TextBuffer::begin_undo_group()/end_undo_group()`), do not invent a parallel undo stack.
implementation_notes: Keep keymap.rs binding-only: prefer putting `line_comment_token` and `LineEditContext` in line_ops.rs. Touch keymap.rs only if the language/tab lookup genuinely must sit next to the action enum.
Use `line_to_byte`/`byte_to_line` for ALL row math; never scan the rope for `\n` manually — that reintroduces the byte/char off-by-one class of bug RISK-002 warned about in MT-001.
Apply per-line edits in DESCENDING line order so lower-offset positions stay valid while higher lines mutate; recompute cursor offsets afterward, then `remove_overlap()` + re-sort.

ToggleComment insert column = the minimum first-non-whitespace column across the affected block, so a commented block lines up (VS Code parity). Uncomment removes the token and one optional following space.
Reuse the language enum from the MT-001 LanguageRegistry (keyed on file extension). Do not define a second competing language type — import/re-export it so a file's language drives the comment token.

`insert_spaces` and `tab_size` come from the operator's editor settings already loaded by the shell/keymap settings layer (MT-010 `keymap_settings.rs` reads `~/.handshake/...`). Plumb them into `LineEditContext`; do not hardcode tab_size=4.
DeleteLine and MoveLine boundary cases (first line, last line, last line without trailing newline) are the highest-bug-density paths — unit test each explicitly.
Each transform is one undo group: wrap in the buffer/panel undo-transaction API if present; if absent, raise BLOCKER-UNDO-API rather than emitting per-edit undo entries.
ports_from_react: app/src/lib/monaco/* — Monaco's built-in line commands (editor.action.commentLine / addCommentLine / removeCommentLine, editor.action.copyLinesDownAction, editor.action.moveLinesUpAction / moveLinesDownAction, editor.action.deleteLines, editor.action.indentLines / outdentLines, editor.action.insertTab) define the exact behaviors and cursor-follow semantics to replicate natively.
binds_backend_api: NONE — pure frontend buffer transforms on TextBuffer (MT-001) and CursorSet (MT-003); no handshake_core API calls, no backend, no clipboard, no filesystem.

risks: RISK-001: Cursor offsets corrupt after a length-changing transform if edits are applied top-down — earlier inserts/deletes invalidate later byte offsets, producing misplaced cursors and garbled text.

RISK-002: Byte vs char off-by-one on multi-byte (UTF-8) lines — computing comment-insert column or indent length in chars but applying in bytes shifts the edit into the middle of a glyph and panics or corrupts the rope.
RISK-003: Per-line undo entries instead of one coalesced entry — a single Ctrl+/ across 5 cursors would require 5 Undos to reverse, breaking VS Code parity and confusing the operator.
RISK-004: ToggleComment ambiguity — a block of mixed commented/uncommented lines toggled inconsistently (some comment, some uncomment) instead of VS Code's all-comment-then-all-uncomment rule.

RISK-005: Boundary bugs — MoveLineUp on line 0, MoveLineDown on the last line, or DeleteLine on the final line without a trailing newline leaving a stray empty line or panicking on out-of-range row math.
RISK-006: Tabs-vs-spaces drift — Indent/InsertTab inserting a literal tab when the file/setting wants spaces (or vice versa), producing mixed indentation the operator did not ask for.
RISK-007: Defining a second language enum divergent from MT-001's LanguageRegistry, so the comment token does not match the file's actual highlight language.
minimum_controls: MC-001: Apply per-line edits in descending line order and recompute every cursor offset post-edit; unit-test multi-cursor transforms on multi-line buffers to confirm cursor positions are exact.
MC-002: Do all column/length math through `line_to_byte`/`byte_to_line` and rope char/byte conversion; include a UTF-8 line (emoji or accented chars) in the test set for ToggleComment and Indent.
MC-003: Assert single-undo coalescing in a dedicated test (AC-007 / PT-003): multi-cursor transform then one Undo returns the exact original buffer.
MC-004: Implement the VS Code all-or-nothing ToggleComment rule and test the mixed-block case explicitly (AC-001).
MC-005: Unit-test every boundary: MoveLineUp@line0 no-op, MoveLineDown@last no-op, DeleteLine@last-row-no-trailing-newline leaves no empty line (AC-002, AC-006).
MC-006: Read `tab_size` and `insert_spaces` from LineEditContext (sourced from operator settings) and test both tab-char and N-spaces modes (AC-004, AC-005).
MC-007: Import/re-export the MT-001 LanguageRegistry language type; forbid a second language enum — a test asserts the comment token for a `.rs` buffer is `//` and for a `.py` buffer is `#`.
